Chiari Malformation 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/chiarimalformation.html 
Chiari Malformation
Also called: Arnold-Chiari Malformation 
Summary 
Chiari malformations (CMs) are structural defects in the cerebellum. The cerebellum is the part of the brain that controls balance. With CM, brain tissue extends into the spinal canal. It can happen when part of the skull is too small, which pushes the brain tissue down. There are several types of CM. One type often happens in children who have neural tube defects .
